meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't debug code
content.seo_description: Learn how to master SaaS pricing and overcome debugging hurdles. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first real-world app.
content.answer_block: To overcome debugging paralysis, treat your SaaS pricing as a logic gate. By modularizing your pricing tiers, you isolate functional dependencies, making code errors predictable and easier to trace. Use Antigravity to structure your model and Claude Code to automate the error-handling logic, turning complex bugs into manageable unit tests.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat, while your code throws errors that make zero sense. We’ve all been there—the 'debugging wall' where your ambition meets the cold, hard reality of syntax errors and logical dead-ends. It feels like you’re stuck, but this is the exact moment where a student transforms into a builder.

The secret isn't just writing more code; it's architecting your features so they are impossible to break. By structuring your SaaS pricing model first, you create a blueprint that forces your logic into clean, testable blocks. This isn't just about revenue; it’s about control. Let’s turn your frustration into a robust, shipping product.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Logic
steps.description: Establish a clear pricing structure (Free, Pro, Enterprise) to dictate your app's feature access. This keeps your code scoped and prevents feature creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust pricing module structure that defines three tiers. Use a dictionary-based approach to map feature flags to specific pricing levels.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a pricing_logic.py file with clear tier definitions and error handling for invalid subscription states'

steps.title: Build the Validator
steps.description: Create a middleware layer that checks if a user has access to a feature before the code executes, preventing runtime errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a decorator function that validates user subscription status before executing protected API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a subscription_validator.py decorator that raises custom exceptions when a user lacks the required tier'

steps.title: Isolate the Debugging Context
steps.description: When a bug occurs, ensure it is contained within the pricing tier logic rather than the core application, making it easier to trace.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current codebase to move pricing-specific logic into isolated modules with clear entry and exit points.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor main.py to move pricing logic into a separate module and add unit tests for each tier'

steps.title: Automate Error Logging
steps.description: Implement automated error reporting that tags bugs by the user's current pricing tier, helping you prioritize fixes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop an error logger that captures the stack trace and the user's subscription tier, outputting to a debug.log file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a global error handler that logs stack traces along with the user's current pricing tier to debug.log'

steps.title: Final Integration Testing
steps.description: Run your final suite to ensure the pricing logic and the application logic are communicating correctly without breaking changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of pytest scripts that simulate different subscription tiers and verify that feature access is restricted correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute pytest on the pricing_logic module to ensure all edge cases are handled'

pro_tips: Always build your pricing model before your database schema to ensure your data relationships support your revenue goals.
Use 'Feature Flags' to toggle functionality based on pricing; it allows you to debug without deleting code.
Treat your error logs as a feature roadmap—the most common errors usually indicate where your users need more clarity.
Never hardcode prices; use a centralized config file or an environment variable to manage tiers dynamically.
When stuck, ask Claude Code to explain the 'why' behind an error, not just the fix; this builds your mental model faster.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing logic is stable, deploy your app to a staging environment and run a smoke test with a mock user for each tier to ensure your access control is air-tight.
content.conversion_block: You have the blueprint and the tools to stop debugging in the dark and start building with confidence. Stop letting errors dictate your progress and start shipping features that matter. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
